name: Billing-to-CRM Drift
description: Subscription changes in the billing system not yet reflected in CRM opportunity records, creating temporary MRR divergence.

name: Multi-Currency Normalization
description: Exchange rate timing differences between billing settlement and CRM currency conversion creating phantom variances.

name: Usage-Based Billing Mismatch
description: Invoiced amounts differ from contracted MRR due to seat overages, Fin AI resolution charges, and Proactive Support message volumes.

name: Contract Amendment Lag
description: Mid-cycle contract changes (early renewals, expansions, co-terms) not yet configured in billing to match what the AE closed in CRM.

name: Credit & Refund Reconciliation
description: Inconsistent rules for whether customer credits, promotional discounts, and partial refunds reduce MRR or only affect collections.

name: Churn Timing Dispute
description: Different teams define churn differently: contract end date (Sales), 90-day unpaid invoice (Finance), or zero usage (Product), causing 3-8% MRR variance.
